text:
We need the smallest positive integer \( n \) such that  
\( 7n \equiv 3 \pmod{11} \).  

The inverse of 7 modulo 11:  
\( 7 \times 8 = 56 \equiv 1 \pmod{11} \), so inverse is 8.  

Multiply both sides by 8:  
\( n \equiv 3 \times 8 = 24 \equiv 2 \pmod{11} \).  

Smallest positive \( n \) is 2.  

Check: \( 7 \times 2 = 14 \equiv 3 \pmod{11} \).  

2
